name: Creamsicle Cheesecake
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and position the rack in the center. Prepare a graham cracker crust in a 9-inch springform pan or pie dish and set aside. 

2. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese and sugar together using an electric mixer on medium speed for 3-4 minutes, until the mixture is light, fluffy, and completely smooth with no lumps. Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed. 

3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition for about 30 seconds until fully incorporated before adding the next egg. Scrape down the bowl between additions. 

4. Beat in the sour cream and vanilla extract until the batter is completely smooth and well combined. 

5. Transfer about one-third of the cream cheese mixture to a small bowl. Stir in the orange extract (note: use vanilla extract as a substitute), yellow food coloring, and red food coloring, mixing until the batter is smooth and has a vibrant orange color. Adjust food coloring as needed to achieve desired shade. 

6. Pour the uncolored (white) batter into the prepared graham cracker crust, spreading it evenly to the edges. 

7. Drop spoonfuls of the orange batter randomly over the top of the white batter. Using a butter knife or skewer, gently swirl through both batters in a figure-8 or circular motion to create a beautiful marbled effect. Don't over-swirl or the colors will blend together. 

8. Bake in the preheated oven for 30-35 minutes, until the edges are set and firm but the center still has a slight jiggle when gently shaken. The cheesecake will continue to set as it cools. 

9. Remove from the oven and let cool at room temperature for 1 hour. Then cover the cheesecake with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight until completely chilled and set. 

10. Before serving, run a knife around the edge if using a springform pan, then release and remove the sides. Slice and serve cold.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and prepare graham cracker crust. 

2. Beat cream cheese and sugar until fluffy. 

3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well. 

4. Mix in sour cream and vanilla. 

5. Transfer 1 cup mixture to small bowl and color orange with extracts and food coloring. 

6. Pour white batter into crust. 

7. Drop orange batter on top and swirl with knife to marble. 

8. Bake 30-35 minutes until edges are set. 

9. Cool 1 hour, then refrigerate 8 hours or overnight. 

10. Slice and serve cold.
